items.hook: ‘Colony’ director Yeon Sang-ho talks sequel plans through novel, video game
items.body: EXCLUSIVE: The film has proved a box office hit in South Korea and is set for a US release on August 28.

items.hook: The rigid airship USS Macon surprised the USS Houston near Clipperton Island with a mail delivery for President Franklin D. Roosevelt, demonstrating its potential for tracking ships at sea.
items.body: USS Macon (ZRS-5) was a rigid airship built and operated by the United States Navy for scouting and served as a "flying aircraft carrier", carrying up to five single-seat Curtiss F9C Sparrowhawk parasite biplanes for scouting or two-seat Fleet N2Y-1s for training. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Return of Tarzan is a novel by American writer Edgar Rice Burroughs, the second in his series of twenty-four books about the title character Tarzan.
items.body: The story was first published in the pulp magazine New Story Magazine in the issues for June through December 1913; the first book edition was published in 1915 by A. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Tunisia imposes a blockade on the French naval base at Bizerte; the French would capture the entire town four days later.
items.body: The Bizerte crisis occurred in July 1961 when Tunisia imposed a blockade on the French naval base at Bizerte, Tunisia, hoping to force its evacuation. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Coronation of George IV of the United Kingdom.
items.body: George IV was King of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland and King of Hanover from 29 January 1820 until his death in 1830. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Black Box is the 25th novel by American crime author Michael Connelly, and the 16th novel featuring Los Angeles Police Department detective Harry Bosch.
items.body: The book was published on 26 November 2012, "in part to honor the 20th anniversary of the character". Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: A car bomb kills Judge Paolo Borsellino and five members of his escort.
items.body: The via D'Amelio bombing was a terrorist attack by the Sicilian Mafia, which took place in Palermo, Sicily, Italy, on 19 July 1992. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Brenda Fricker, Oscar winner for My Left Foot, dies aged 81
items.body:
The acclaimed Irish actor started her career in Coronation Street and Casualty before a string of high-profile Hollywood roles

Brenda Fricker, who became the first female Irish Oscar winner for acting with My Left Foot, has died ag

items.hook: ESA’s Mars Express meets NASA’s Psyche
Using AI for creative pursuits? Moderation is key
items.body: An experiment showed AI users had the most creative ideas when they used it in moderation – not too much and not too little. Columnist David Robson puts the finding to the test, and explores what we lose when we over-rely on AI

items.hook: Bayeux Tapestry in 'wonderful state' after journey to London, French minister says
items.body: The French culture minister has told the BBC that the Bayeux Tapestry is in "a wonderful state".

items.hook: The attempt to install Lady Jane Grey as Queen of England collapses after only nine days.
items.body: Lady Jane Grey, also known as Lady Jane Dudley after her marriage, and nicknamed as the "Nine Days Queen", was an English noblewoman who was proclaimed Queen of England and Ireland on 10 July 1553 and reigned until she was deposed by the Privy Council of England, which proclaimed her cousin, Mary I, as the new Queen on 19 July. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Hello is a 2008 Indian Hindi-language comedy thriller film directed by Atul Agnihotri, starring Sharman Joshi, Sohail Khan, Gul Panag, Isha Koppikar, Amrita Arora, and Sharat Saxena in the lead roles.
items.body: The film is based on Chetan Bhagat's novel, One Night @ the Call Center. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Neramu Siksha is a 2009 Indian Telugu-language legal drama film directed by Vijaya Nirmala starring herself, Krishna, Jayasudha, Akul Balaji, Dheeraj and Akarsha.
items.body: It is based on the novel Vala by Bollimuntha Nageswara Rao. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Red Mouth by Sheila Armstrong review – profound exploration of Ireland’s deep time
items.body:
Sinister bogland discoveries haunt the intersecting lives of four characters in this meditative, exquisitely written tale

items.hook: A Game of Thrones: The Board Game is a strategy board game created by Christian T.
items.body: Petersen and published by Fantasy Flight Games in 2003. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Italian War of 1542–46: The first Siege of Boulogne begins.
items.body: The Italian War of 1542–1546 was a conflict late in the Italian Wars, pitting Francis I of France and Suleiman I of the Ottoman Empire against the Holy Roman Emperor Charles V and Henry VIII of England. One of those things you can't un-know.
